perturbation.original_text: The obligations undertaken by each party pursuant to this Agreement shall remain in effect for three years from the last date of disclosure of Confidential Information, and shall survive any termination or expiration hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations undertaken by each party pursuant to this Agreement shall remain in effect until either party provides written notice of termination, at which point all obligations cease immediately. The obligations do not survive any termination or expiration hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the duration of the confidentiality obligations, contradicting the original three-year term. It introduces immediate termination upon notice, contrasting with the original clause that obligations continue for three years and survive termination or expiration of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations undertaken by each party pursuant to this Agreement shall remain in effect until either party provides written notice of termination, at which point all obligations cease immediately. The obligations do not survive any termination or expiration hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of confidentiality obligations. The original text stated the obligations remain in effect for three years from the last date of disclosure, and survive termination. The changed text states obligations cease immediately upon written notice and do not survive termination. This directly contradicts the original three-year term and survival clause. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which means the contradiction exists as the text now contradicts the original statement.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing party, and upon the written request of either party, the other party shall promptly return to the disclosing party all Confidential Information disclosed to it and all copies thereof or at the disclosing party's option shall destroy all such Confidential Information and shall provide the receiving party with a certificate that all Confidential Information has been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information shall become the shared property of both parties. Upon termination of this agreement, neither party is obligated to return or destroy any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration transforms the ownership of Confidential Information and removes the return/destroy obligation. The original clause states it remains the property of the disclosing party and must be returned or destroyed upon request. This revised clause states it becomes shared property and there's no obligation to return/destroy anything upon termination which creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information shall become the shared property of both parties. Upon termination of this agreement, neither party is obligated to return or destroy any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The contradiction text is identical to the changed text. The change transforms confidential information from being the property of the disclosing party, to being the shared property of both parties. It also removes the return/destroy obligation which inherently is contradictory to the original text of ownership and obligation.
